textByHash.29860aff4c54797b13e1028289f236b909f307ed: It's nice to meet you here.
textByHash.2910ee5df2c8047026de6d808a27702f2e003b97: I'm Chuck. I used to exchange logs for certificates, so people could trade them in bulk more conveniently. Certificates were what we used before banks automatically issued banknotes for most tradeable items.
textByHash.d1810fd8b713644d5f209c62b3096f1e0ea6741c: Even today, banknotes are sometimes referred to as 'certs', in memory of the certificates we used before.
textByHash.4a77cc3e65d9ff6b4822f91d4b553411e8058f84: I had colleagues who'd exchange other items for certificates - Niles, Miles and Giles were the most famous of us. But those three found new jobs rather than retiring like I did.
textByHash.8da88160a2e115cad9a0b0212d1af7d064d437fc: No, I don't think so. The big high-street banks made my work useless, and I don't fancy joining Niles, Miles and Giles on their weird treks.
textByHash.ba4519ebd15b0889638d54944ae9792170809ddb: No, I'll stay here and enjoy my retirement. It's nice of you to ask, though.
textByHash.a18cdef92247edb085f4ff79069ec8403fb272aa: If you want that converted into a banknote, take it to a bank - banks made my work useless, so they can deal with your requests now. I've retired.
textByHash.216e1bf2924ef31abc89a1deac594e73dee2e83c: If you want your banknotes converted, take them to a bank - banks made my work useless, so they can deal with your requests now. I've retired.
textByHash.c0efbd4523d29057a3e389d3d9ad8edce0572ad1: Even in your so-called modern era there's no such thing as a banknote for that. Hah!
